_note: Job descriptions for roles applied to. Add new JDs here when provided.

cardinal-energy/field-operations-summer-.title: Field Operations Summer Student
cardinal-energy/field-operations-summer-.description: Cardinal Energy Ltd. is an upstream oil & gas company with assets in Alberta, Saskatchewan, and British Columbia. With a track record of sustainable energy practices and innovation in the oil and gas sector over the past 10+ years. We are looking for a student currently enrolled in post secondary school that is interested in a joining the oil and gas industry to work as part of an active, multi disciplinary team supporting the growth opportunities within our Business Units. Preference will be given to students currently enrolled in a relevant engineering or technical school program. Key Responsibilities: Assist operations staff with daily field activities. Support data collection and production reporting. Participate in optimization reviews and safety meetings. Help with minor maintenance tasks and special projects. Learn industry best practices, with a focus on safety and compliance. Requirements: Strong interest in oil and gas operations. Willingness to learn and take initiative. Effective communication and teamwork skills. Currently enrolled in a Canadian post-secondary undergraduate or diploma program.

health-shared-services/it-student.title: Co-op Work Experience Student - IT
health-shared-services/it-student.description: Your Opportunity: Health Shared Services provides vital services to clinical and non-clinical teams across Alberta's healthcare system to help them deliver timely, high-quality healthcare to patients and families across the province. We work alongside provincial healthcare agencies and corporations, providing expertise, systems and resources related to: Finance, contracting, procurement, supply management, and capital management. Data and analytics, information technology, clinical informatics, and health information management. Human resources, workplace health and safety, health professions, and medical affairs. Privacy, legal, ethics and compliance and internal audit. Within Health Shared Services, the Information Technology team is a very dynamic and driven group who engage in complex, challenging and diverse projects to deliver collective results. Through quality work and innovative solutions, we meet the ever-changing needs of healthcare for Albertans. This is a four-month term: May-August 2026. Applicants must be current students returning to studies in September 2026. Description: Students will become active contributors within their assigned teams and will receive hands on training and mentorship from experienced IT professionals. Placements focus on delivering technology services, supporting project execution, and contributing to applications and systems that enhance clinical and operational processes. Example roles include: Junior Help Desk/Customer Support, Junior Web Development, Junior Project Support, Junior Business Analyst Support, Health Information Management Support. Required Qualifications: Currently enrolled in a CO-OP or work experience program in an IT, Science, Engineering, or Business-related field. Demonstrated ability to work both independently and collaboratively within a professional team environment. Confident and professional communication skills. Self-motivated, proactive, and accountable. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills. Proficiency with Microsoft 365 applications including Excel, SharePoint, Word, Visio, Teams, Project, and PowerPoint. Experience with programming languages such as Python, Java, C, or C#. Additional technical skills in Power BI, Tableau, SQL, machine learning, data analysis, data analytics, or business intelligence.

bis-safety-software/software-tester-qa-q.title: Software Tester (QA/QC) Intern
bis-safety-software/software-tester-qa-q.description: About the Role: During your internship with us, you will join our Technology Team and contribute to the quality and reliability of our software. You will work alongside our QA team to execute manual test cases, identify defects before they reach our clients, and contribute to the development of our automated testing practices. We want to lean into your strengths in software development and AI as you get started with us in this role. This internship runs May through August, with the potential for continued part-time work during the school year and full-time work in future summers, based on performance and business need, and has the potential to lead to a full-time role within our software development team. This is an in-person role in our Sherwood Park, AB office. What you'll do: Execute structured and exploratory manual test cases to identify defects across our software platform before release. Document test results, defect reports, and test coverage clearly and consistently. Review software requirements and develop test scenarios that reflect real-world use cases. Investigate bugs and defects to understand root causes and suggest practical fixes or process improvements. Collaborate with developers, product managers, and UI/UX designers to communicate findings and support efficient resolution. Contribute to our automated testing practices by applying your development skills to help build, improve, and maintain automated test frameworks. Explore how AI tools and techniques can be applied to improve test coverage, defect detection, and testing efficiency. You might be the right fit if you: Have a background in software development and are comfortable reading and writing code. Have a genuine interest in or experience with AI. Are naturally detail-oriented. Think systematically and document your work clearly. Are curious, collaborative, adaptable, and take initiative. Bonus: Basic Playwright knowledge, including an understanding of UI locators and JavaScript/TypeScript fundamentals. $21-$23 an hour. About BIS: BIS Safety Software has been headquartered in Sherwood Park, Alberta, since 2006. We build software that helps organizations manage safety training, learning, and compliance.

fortisbc/information-systems-coop.title: Information Systems Co-op
fortisbc/information-systems-coop.description: Affiliation: MoveUP. Employment Status: Co-op Student. Salary: $24.59 Hourly. Temp Duration: 4 or 8 month term. Posting End Date: Open until filled. As the province's largest energy provider with more than 100 years of knowledge and experience, we proudly deliver renewable energy, natural gas, electricity and propane to 1.3 million customers. We're actively seeking new talent to join our mission of delivering a stronger energy future in B.C. As one of BC's Top Employers with a diverse team of over 2,800 employees, we are committed to a safe and inclusive culture where each of us can connect, belong, and grow. Responsibilities: Under the direction of the Manager, IS Applications, this co-op student will be responsible for: Assist in the monitoring of production systems (including application logs). Identify and resolve problems in order to prevent negative impacts to application users. Review, analyze and update software application documentation including architectural drawings using Microsoft Visio. Communicate effectively and demonstrate professional competence with users, co-workers and management. Perform software analysis and requirement gathering along with some minor build and test activities. Requirements: Currently enrolled in an undergrad Computer Information Systems, Software Engineering, or equivalent program. Enrolled in a co-op program. Familiar and able to work with various Microsoft products such as Word, Excel, SharePoint and Visio. Software development training. Valid class 5 BC driver's license. Additional Information: This is a full-time temporary student co-op opportunity with a 4 or 8 month term starting in May 2026.

rakuten-kobo/devops-engineering-coop.title: DevOps Engineering Co-op
rakuten-kobo/devops-engineering-coop.description: Here at Rakuten Kobo Inc., our mission is to make reading lives better. We stand for readers. We also stand for our employees and support their growth and development. We offer a start-up environment, interesting and engaging work within the technology and publishing industries, and a group of friendly and talented individuals. The Role: A unique DevOps engineering opportunity to join a lean, impactful team and actively develop tools and workflows to significantly increase build and release efficiencies. This role provides hands-on exposure and a chance to interact with various groups within Kobo's large research and development team. We are currently looking for a proactive and dedicated 3rd or 4th-year university student to join our DevOps team at our Toronto office for a 12-16 month co-op term starting May 2026. Key Responsibilities: Develop and maintain cloud deployment pipelines and workflows. Actively contribute to the development and maintenance of our continuous integration/deployment pipelines. Develop scripts to automate various operational tasks and system integrations. Write and maintain Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using tools like Terraform/Chef cookbooks. Write and maintain monitoring scripts and tools to ensure system health and performance. Collaborate with development teams to understand requirements and implement efficient deployment strategies. Required Skills: Currently enrolled in a 3rd or 4th year of a Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a closely related technical program. Foundational understanding of Cloud-based technologies (GCP, AWS, Azure). Experience with GitOps/IaC practices. Proficiency in scripting languages such as Terraform, Ansible, Bash, Python, or comparable. Demonstrated experience with REST APIs and system integrations. Strong troubleshooting skills. Preferred Skills: Experience with Google Cloud Platform (GCP). Experience in continuous integration/deployment tools (GitHub Actions, ArgoCD, Helm, Chef). Experience with container technologies (Docker, Kubernetes). Experience with various web servers (Nginx, Apache). Experience with different Operating Systems (Windows, Linux). Exposure to working in an Agile software development environment. Salary: $50,000.00 CAD.

cenovus/information-technology-student.title: Student, AI & Data Science
cenovus/information-technology-student.description: This posting is for all Computer Science, Data Science student positions in multiple areas. Possible placement includes: Student, AI, Software Development & Data Science; Student, Reporting & Analytics; Student, Data Engineering. Start Date: September 2026. Length of Term: 4 Months or 8 Months. Location: Calgary. Job Post End Date: 03/16/2026. What you'll do - Responsibilities may vary depending on the specific position assigned. Student, AI, Software Development & Data Science: Design and development of data solutions. Development and refinement of ML models. Directly engaging with team and stakeholders in the ownership of data solution. Managing own workloads and communications with stakeholders. Who you are: Legally authorized to work in Canada full-time for the duration of your work term. Available to begin work in September 2026. Ability to work well in a team environment. Solid analytical and Microsoft Suite skills, specifically Excel. Excellent interpersonal, judgement, and decision-making skills. Education: Must be enrolled in a Computer Science or Data Science, Business, Economics, or Engineering Program at an accredited post-secondary institution at the time of intended work term and returning to full-time studies after the work term. New graduates are not eligible for student opportunities. Student Program Benefits: A multi-discipline program with meaningful, hands-on work experience. Networking events. Wide support network including direct leaders, program leads, human resource, peers, mentor(s). Structured goal setting and performance evaluations. Opportunity to present work-term learnings to the Cenovus Leadership team.

critical-mass/development-intern.title: Development Intern
critical-mass/development-intern.description: Critical Mass' Internship opportunities are part of our Trailhead Program and provide a 12-week jump-start to your career. The program is designed to fast-track your growth through regular learning opportunities, mentorship, and hands-on client work - all while earning a competitive stipend. Our internship will run from May 26 to August 14. As a development intern you will get to work with a tech lead to gain experience to all the different aspects of the technology discipline at Critical Mass. You will gain experience seeing how the technology team works with different departments like creative and UX to create top-notch user experiences. You Will: Gain exposure to all elements of the technology discipline at Critical Mass. Understand how technology fits into the Critical Mass agency offerings. Collaborate with other interns on a client project and our global intern project. Deliver solutions balancing technical and business requirements. Understand business and user requirements. Maintain high coding standards and practices to exercise quality control on all aspects of web development. Problem-solve in all areas of web development. You Have: Demonstrated development experience with hand-coding advanced HTML, CSS, HTML5, CSS3, and JavaScript through class or certificate program. Demonstrated development exposure to React or VUE. Package manager and automation tool experience (npm, gulp, grunt). Basic understanding of Responsive Web Design principles. Proficiency with Semantic HTML Markup. Aptitude to collaborate and work with cross discipline teams. Exposure in diagnosing and solving cross-browser and cross-platform issues. Currently in 2nd or 3rd year of a 4-year degree or 1st year of a 2-year specialization program. Nice-to-Have: Working knowledge of Python, with the ability to write, test, and debug basic scripts. Exposure to AI concepts and practical application through coursework or personal projects.

university-of-calgary/grc-student.title: Governance, Risk & Compliance Student
university-of-calgary/grc-student.description: The Information Technology is currently looking for a Student to join the Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC) team. This candidate will be able to work 35 hrs during off-school periods and part time during school periods for up to 22 hrs. GRC focuses on developing, enhancing, and maintaining end-to-end processes in compliance with control frameworks for managing the University's information and related technologies. The incumbent ensures that core processes, controls and compliance requirements align to the needs of the University, providing leadership and technical guidance to both IT and Business Units on the identification and mitigation of technology risk. Primary Purpose: The successful candidate will help execute cyber security risk assessments of the University of Calgary suppliers. You will provide specialized expertise and guidance on assessing risks, identifying potential gaps, and you will also be required to maintain the risk acceptance tracking process and maintain residual risks in the cyber security risk register while tracking remediation activities with key business and technical stakeholders. Accountabilities: Assist in tracking and monitoring status of information security risks (policy exceptions and non-conformities). Perform risk assessments reviews related to vendor risk management. Assist with annual reviews of mission critical vendors. Assist with data analysis, documentation, and ad-hoc projects related to governance, compliance, and risk management topics. Provide support for audit preparedness. Track status on Information Security Policy and Process annual reviews. Handle sensitive and/or confidential material and information with suitable discretion. Qualifications: Actively pursuing a bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Cyber Security, Information Systems, Information Security and Privacy, Technology Management, Software security or related field at the University of Calgary. A general understanding of networks, operating systems, cloud operations and associated technologies and services. Knowledge and/or understanding of common industry frameworks, including NIST Cyber Security Framework (CSF), NIST 800-53, ISO 27001 and 27002, PCI DSS, or OWASP is considered an asset. Must have advanced ability in the use of Microsoft Office which includes Access, Excel, Word, and PowerPoint. Strong organizational, process development and documentation skills. Ability to communicate clearly and concisely to diverse audiences, in both oral and written form. Application Deadline: March 11, 2026.

clio/software-developer-coop.title: Software Developer, Co-op
clio/software-developer-coop.description: Clio is the global leader in legal AI technology. Seeking a Software Developer Co-op for an 8-month term (May 2026 - December 2026) on the Engineering team in Toronto. You will work on a SaaS product gaining experience with Ruby on Rails, AngularJS, React Native, MySQL, Redis, and ElasticSearch. Responsibilities: Design, develop, and deploy high-quality software. Contribute to technical scoping and planning. Communicate project progress and address impediments. Collaborate across the development organization. Participate in thorough code reviews. Drive implementation of new features and timely solutions for customer-impacting issues. Actively participate in team-level and company-wide initiatives. Contribute to coordinating product support through rotational assignment. Commit to continuous professional development. Qualifications: Completed second year coursework, 3rd or 4th year Computer Science/Engineering student. Rational and critical thinker. Excellent CS fundamentals: data structures, algorithms, programming languages, SQL. Superb written and verbal communication skills. Keen interest in improving craft using AI. Bonus: bringing new ideas, experimenting and learning outside school curriculum. Compensation: $67,000 CAD. Hybrid work with minimum 2 days in office.

nokia/innovation-automation.title: Innovation & Automation Co-op
nokia/innovation-automation.description: Nokia is a global leader in connectivity for the AI era. Seeking an Innovation & Automation Co-op/Intern for a 12-month term (May 2026 - April 2027), based on-site at 600 March Rd, Ottawa. Responsibilities: Analyze existing processes to identify automation opportunities and document automated workflows. Research emerging GenAI technologies and assess their potential applications within CNS Services & Care. Assist in developing proof-of-concepts and pilot projects leveraging GenAI solutions. Support the integration of GenAI technologies into existing systems and business processes. Learn and apply customer-centric design principles, including mapping customer journeys and identifying pain points. Contribute to the conceptualization of new service delivery models and architectural improvements. Gain exposure to cybersecurity, data privacy, and compliance best practices, and support related assessments. Participate in ideation, market research, technology/vendor evaluations, and provide project coordination support. Qualifications: Bachelor's degree candidate in Computer Science, Engineering, Information Technology, Business Administration, or related field at an accredited Canadian school. Strong academic record with excellent analytical and problem-solving skills. Basic understanding of programming concepts (Python, JavaScript); automation tools or scripting experience is a plus. Strong communication and interpersonal skills. Proficiency in English. Nice to have: curiosity about AI and automation, proactive self-starter, demonstrated interest in technology and innovation.

deloitte/technology-transformation.title: Technology Transformation (Bloom Scholarship)
deloitte/technology-transformation.description: Deloitte Bloom 2027 Scholarship - Technology Transformation work term targeting Engineering, AI & Data practice. The Bloom program is Deloitte's hiring stream for neurodivergent candidates. Work term available Winter 2027, Summer 2027, or Fall 2027, any Canadian office. The Engineering, AI & Data practice focuses on helping clients build, modernize, and transform enterprise technology systems - including digital solutions, analytics, technology enablement, and enterprise system transformations. Candidates should have hands-on experience with production systems, CI/CD, testing, backend development, and integrations. Strong interest in enterprise transformation, digital modernization, and professional certifications such as CISA is valued. Cross-functional collaboration, stakeholder communication, and technical documentation skills are essential.

kpmg/advisory-consulting.title: Calgary Internship, Management Consulting (Fall 2026)
kpmg/advisory-consulting.description: KPMG Advisory Management Consulting Intern/Co-op, Calgary. 4-month term starting September 2026. Service lines available: People & Change (strategic alignment, workforce shaping, digital HR transformation, culture shifting), Enterprise Solutions (Microsoft, Oracle, SAP, Salesforce, ServiceNow, Workday - end-to-end system implementations and technology transformations), and Delivery Services (integrated human-centered approach to major projects, program and project management for large-scale transformation programs). What they look for: Enrolled in university degree (Science, Math, Engineering, CS, Business, Commerce, Arts, etc.). Second, third, or fourth year of study. Highly motivated, enthusiastic about variety. Excellent communication skills. Strong academic record. Ability to work independently and in a team. Strong problem solving and analytical abilities. Entrepreneurial drive and creative problem solving. Success factors: building collaborative relationships, developing and motivating others, learning and adapting, driving quality, fostering innovation.

aecon/data-systems-analyst.title: Data and Systems Analyst Co-op
aecon/data-systems-analyst.description: Aecon Nuclear Data and Systems Analyst Co-op. Location: Cambridge, ON. 12-month co-op term. Operating Sector: Nuclear. The role provides ongoing support to the business unit in data analytics, software support, and electronic work packages for nuclear projects during planning, training, and execution windows. Includes ongoing risk analysis and reporting in safety, quality, production, and cost. Responsibilities: Collect, manage, and interpret data to identify trends. Develop series modeling with automated workflows. Apply statistical analysis and process analysis techniques for planning, training, and execution phases. Support projects in identifying process improvement and digital transformation opportunities. Develop reports, procedures, and processes. Provide ongoing support to clients and project teams. Liaise with vendors for new software development, troubleshooting, maintenance, and upgrades. Develop and deliver training to the nuclear division on new software and technologies. Act as subject matter expert in one or two commonly used nuclear division software. Independently plan, schedule, and manage own time. Requirements: 3rd or 4th year Computer Science or Engineering degree. Min GPA 3.0. Power BI, Power Apps, Power Automate, Excel experience. Database design skills. Statistical analysis and process analysis techniques. Excellent interpersonal and communication skills. Salary: $22-$28/hour.

ey/technology-risk.title: Assurance Technology Risk Intern/Co-op 2026
ey/technology-risk.description: EY Assurance Technology Risk Intern/Co-op. Location: Edmonton (also Toronto, Vancouver, Ottawa, Waterloo, London). Terms: September 2026, January 2027, May 2027. The Technology Risk team, sometimes described as IT Audit, is focused on helping clients better manage their risks, reducing exposure to negative events to successfully turn risk into results. Work with clients to manage risks in areas such as design and operation of internal controls, security of business-critical systems, delivery of major IT-enabled programs, third-party relationships, and management of business-critical data. This team supports CPA study. Hours worked count towards CPA designation. Responsibilities: Client-facing work demonstrating professionalism and competence. Be responsive to client requests and develop productive working relationships with client personnel. Stay informed of current business/economic developments relevant to clients. Collaborate with team members to set goals and responsibilities. What they look for: Agile, growth-oriented mindset. Curious and purpose driven. Pursuing undergraduate or graduate degree in business, STEM, or related major. Advanced written and verbal communication skills. Strong dedication to teamwork. Commitment to integrity within a professional environment. Salary: Toronto $56,000-$66,000; other locations $53,000-$63,000.

mackenzie/mas-intern.title: Intern 2026 Investment Management - MAS
mackenzie/mas-intern.description: Mackenzie Investments MAS Intern - Multi-Asset Strategies Team. Fall term September to December 2026, Toronto office (hybrid, 3 days/week). Work with and be mentored by a Python and Pandas expert. Collaborate through pair programming and test-driven development. Use code generation (LibCST and Fixit) and other automation to improve codebase quality. Responsibilities: Replace nested loops with vectorized Pandas operations or convert Pandas to Polars. Introduce modular utility functions to reduce duplication. Add unit tests to functions where absent. Use characterization tests to ensure output consistency for unchanged inputs. Optimize data loading routines for large files. Clean up legacy code to follow PEP8 and readability standards. Requirements: Strong academic performance in CompSci, Engineering, Commerce, Economics, or Math. Familiarity with Python, motivated to deepen programming expertise. Basic understanding of Pandas or other data-centric libraries. Curious about solving large-scale data problems in code. Strong interest in financial markets with ability to interpret economic news and market conditions. Strong interpersonal and communication skills. Salary range: $49,000-$51,000 prorated.

kinaxis/software-developer-intern.title: Software Developer Intern
kinaxis/software-developer-intern.description: Kinaxis Software Developer Intern - Maestro web client team. Location: Ottawa, Canada (hybrid, 3 days/week in office). Term: 4 or 6 months starting May 2026. Responsibilities: Develop high quality code for the Maestro web client and application server. Enhance product quality by fixing bugs, writing tests, and improving product performance. Grow design system by contributing to reusable component library. Collaborate with development, QA, and UX teams in delivering new features. What they're looking for: Currently enrolled in software development program. Experience building web applications, personal projects on GitHub. Open mindset for learning and improving. Strong problem-solving and communication skills. Preferred: TypeScript, JavaScript, C#. A front-end framework like React, Vue, or Angular. Unit testing, end-to-end testing. Functional programming. CSS, CSS in JS. REST APIs. Compensation: $25.24 - $37.02 hourly.

sap/data-platform-technical-support.title: SAP iXp Intern - Data and Platform, Technical Support
sap/data-platform-technical-support.description: SAP iXp Intern - Data and Platform, Technical Support. Location: Vancouver, BC. Start: May 2026. Contract: 8 months, full-time. Responsibilities: Investigate and resolve critical system issues for Enterprise level Fortune 500 companies. Analyze complex, real world, unique problems; often working in production environments. Master SAP software used by industries, governments, and multinational companies. What you bring: Bachelor's in Computer Science or related field. Excellent English communication, diagnostic, and technical analysis skills. Strong team player with collaborative mindset for a global team. Enjoy working directly with customers through phone, chat, screensharing and case management. Prior customer service or technical support experience preferred. Experience with: Databases (SAP HANA, Oracle, DB2, SQL Server, MySQL). Operating systems (Windows Server, Linux, command line). Data communications and networking (HTTP, TCP/IP, DNS). RESTful web services and JSON. Security and Authentication (SAML, Kerberos, SSL, SSO). SAP ERP experience beneficial.

deloitte/it-audit-assurance.title: Analyst, IT Audit & Assurance Co-op/Intern 2027
deloitte/it-audit-assurance.description: Analyst - IT Audit & Assurance - Co-op/Intern 2027 - Vancouver. Job Type: Co-op/Intern. Work Model: Hybrid. Primary Location: Vancouver, BC. Within the Assurance practice, IT Audit & Assurance seeks to elevate Trust, Integrity and Confidence in Canadian organizations by Assuring and Advising on IT and emerging technology risks to help our clients achieve regulatory, internal and extended enterprise requirements and maximize their impact. Within the IT Audit & Assurance (IT A&A) practice, you can expect to have a dynamic and exciting role that is full of challenges and opportunities to grow. We perform audits of general IT controls as part of financial statement audits, and third-party assurance (such as SOC 1 and SOC 2 audits). A typical day might involve working with your team to assess technology processes and identifying areas of risk. You might then work with a client to execute an audit plan that addresses those risks and ensures compliance with the audit objectives. You could be involved in conducting fieldwork, which may include interviews with clients, reviewing documentation, and testing controls. You may also be responsible for drafting audit findings and discussing issues with management, which requires strong communication skills. We welcome applications from individuals who are passionate about controls, audit methodologies, business and IT operations and IT risk. This role offers an excellent opportunity to gain valuable experience while working towards a CPA, CIA, or CISA designation. Expected qualifications: Demonstrated leader who takes initiative. Outstanding communication skills, flexibility, humility, integrity, and professionalism. Creative thinker excited by innovation. Preferred areas of academic study: Business/Commerce/Accounting, Engineering/Computer Science or related fields. Salary range: $48,000 - $58,700.
